title: Board Snapshots / Automated Exports
excerpt:
This may seem like 2 requests, but the end-goal is similar enough. 
It would be great to have a means of seeing what a board looked like at a certain point in time. The idea is a board goes through many changes, and rath…

posts.author: jaykjapra (Jay Japra)
posts.cookedHtml:
This may seem like 2 requests, but the end-goal is similar enough.

It would be great to have a means of seeing what a board looked like at a certain point in time. The idea is a board goes through many changes, and rather than having to sort through the activity log, I could just set which day or iteration I want to see the board looked like in comparison to today (or any other point).

If this is too difficult, the easiest workaround I can think of is the ability to set up timed exports on a regular cadence so that the snapshots are automatically captured as Excel files. For now we can remember to manually export, but hey, why not automate?

The best comparison I can think of is Google Sheets version control in which you can revert back to a sheet from a certain point in time. I’m not asking for the ability to fully revert, just a chance to preview what the board looked like. I imagine this data is kept somewhere for a certain amount of time?

posts.author: lrobert (Ludovic ROBERT)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I would really like something like that.
I need a feature to export (to Excel or whatever) items before the are archived. For example, I want an automation to export all items marked as “Done” once a month and then archive them.

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: Also looking to export to excel on a schedule automatically.

posts.author: Rhi (Rhi Leedam)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this is a feature I really need! Automatic excel exports each month, before the info is archived or cleared.

posts.author: Tricatrica (Tricia Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: I came here to ask about this. We are also wanting to automatically export data from a board on a recurring basis. Any format would be awesome. Just something we can share with others. It would be even cooler if it you could give it a couple of email addresses to receive the export. Thanks!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t currently have this need. But, it would be relatively easy to copy a board to Google Sheets or an Excel workbook and email the file to people on a schedule using Integromat.

Another option would be duplicate the board and email links. Again, easy in Integromat.

posts.author: Tricatrica (Tricia Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve never used Integromat. I’ll have to check them out. Thanks!

posts.author: agalvao (Ana)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I am also looking to export to excel on a schedule automatically. If anyone has done some work-around for this, please share!

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml: @agalvao @Tricatrica @Elliot just wanted to let you know that with the Screenful add-on that you can use to schedule exports daily/weekly/monthly in PDF or CSV format and have it automatically sent to the specified email addresses. You can learn more at monday.com reports: Advanced custom report & analytics for monday.com — Screenful

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Throwing in another solution here after two years, since I bet it can be still useful for anyone stumbling across this topic.

With Spreadsheet Gorilla you can quite easily send emails including a custom email text and attached Excel files (based on your board data) with automations on a daily, weekly or monthly basis – all done without even touching your board.

We are currently working on custom filters that will let you strip down the exported board data to e.g. including only items with STATUS is DONE. Expect them being shipped within the next week.

– Markus from Gorilla Apps

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jaykjapra ,
You’re actually describing something that Board Email Reports can already do! Instead of digging through the activity log, our app lets you automate regular reports that will show the progress made over time.

  • You can schedule reports to be sent at set intervals, so you always compare of what the board looked like at different points in time.
  • The reports come in xls-format, making it easy to compare changes and reuse data.
  • You get to choose which columns to track, so you’re only keeping the data that matters.
  • And the best part? You don’t have to invite users as guests as guests to your monday.com board—just enter their email in the automation, and they’ll get the reports.

It’s not full version history like Google Sheets, but it helps to understand the progress made over time, so you always have a way to look back at changes. This an example of the email and the report in it for the Costs changes column:

image440×505 22 KB

image1275×663 65 KB

Just mention other users, that I the app works for: @lrobert , @Elliot , @Rhi , @Tricatrica , @agalvao.

posts.author: claire.sargeant (Claire)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi can this be exported as a PDF and display the changes side by side rather than in a table?

And can it display images as well (again side by side) - we have a user case of needing to review before and after pictures.
